channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: It’s Time For Sony To Show Its Cards: Watch The Big Summer PlayStation State Of Play Right Here
item.content:encoded:
Who’s ready to see some games?! Today’s Summer Game Fest–adjacent PlayStation State of Play is the longest Sony showcase in a bit and gives the console maker a chance to set the stage for the final years of the PS5. We’ve already been promised a deep dive into September’s Wolverine game from Insomniac, but all eyes are on updates to what the rest of Sony’s first-party studios have been working on. Will we maybe even get a tease for something? Who’s read for a new Ape Escape?

You can watch below:

Rumors are already swirling about a reveal for the worst-kept secret at Santa Monica Studios, which is reportedly a new God of War spin-off featuring Freya. Expect big announcements from third-party partners as well. Will Sony snag the long-awaited reveal for Final Fantasy VII Remake part three, or will that honor go to Geoff Keighley’s Summer Game Fest showcase later in the week, as it did back in 2022?

Sound off in the comments with what you hope to see!



https://kotaku.com/its-time-for-sony-to-show-its-cards-watch-the-big-summer-playstation-state-of-play-right-here-2000701636
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fallout 76 Is Finally Getting A Next-Gen Upgrade For PS5 And Xbox Series X/S Nearly Eight Years Later
item.content:encoded:
Can a new Forza Horizon competitor deliver some of the breezy spectacle that may have been missing from the latest entry? How come it took Fallout 76 so long to get a PS5 and Xbox Series X/S version? What will make one of the characters in the new Silent Hill drop an F-bomb? It’s your Kotaku Checkpoint roundup for June 2, 2026, where we are still finding catharsis in yesterday’s 60 Minutes town hall meltdown. But onto more fun things.

Let’s remember some of the coolest SNES-era save spots:

Save points in SNES RPGs ❤️

Shown here:
1. Tales of Phantasia
2. Final Fantasy VI (FFIII in NA)
3. Final Fantasy V
4. Secret of Mana
5. Super Mario RPG
6. Chrono Trigger
7. Trials of Mana/Seiken Densetsu 3
8. Breath of Fire
9. Star Ocean pic.twitter.com/Muf00nNEI6

— FantasyAnime (@FantasyAnime) June 2, 2026

Fallout 76 is about to look prettier on Xbox Series X than PS5

Bethesda announced the very long-awaited next-gen upgrade for the survival MMO on Tuesday and the specs include up to 4K resolution on Xbox Series X, but only up to 1440p on PS5 and PS5 Pro. Weird! Still no word on when we’re getting cross-play.

“Available to everyone who owns Fallout 76,” Bethesda wrote today. “You will be able to download the Xbox Series X/S and PlayStation 5 test versions from the Xbox and PlayStation Stores. Once testing is completed, we plan on releasing the update later this summer!”

Resident Evil might go to Japan in the future

“I think that every Japanese Resident Evil fan has definitely thought about a Japan setting, and I’ve also considered it myself,” Resident Evil Requiem producer Masato Kumazawa said in an interview with Futaman (translated by IGN). “As the series is primarily developed by staff in Japan, I think everyone on the dev team has probably thought about it. Even though Japan hasn’t appeared as a setting for the games yet, I think it might at some point in the future.”

There’s a new open-world racing game

It’s called Clutch and it’s the debut project from former Forza Horizon creative director Mike Brown. Per VGC, the game features a story written by Skins co-creator Jamie Brittain and will focus on a pair of siblings competing in a prestigious racing series and a collective of car enthusiasts called the Midnight Club. It’s being made in Unreal Engine 5 with a focus on showing off the unique details of each car.

Hell Let Loose: Vietnam has been delayed

The 50v50 multiplayer shooter was supposed to arrive this month. It’ll be out August 13 instead. “We want Hell Let Loose: Vietnam to deliver the best possible experience for players on both PC and console. Following intensive internal testing, coupled with the open beta feedback, we’ve identified several areas where additional development time will allow us to deliver a stronger launch experience,” the developers wrote in a new blog post.

StarCraft 2 just became “essentially a new game”

That’s according to some players who were testing out the recent new patch for Blizzard’s RTS (via GameSpot). Update 5.0.16 includes significant updates to the game’s units and economy, as well as quality-of-life improvements. Who needs StarCraft 3, right?

Silent Hill: Townfall features an F-bomb

“The word “f**k” is heard in the game.” That little detail was leaked in the ESRB age rating for the upcoming horror spin-off. It’s believed to be a PS5 exclusive. Maybe it’ll be at one of this week’s showcases.

ICYMI:

  • There’s a “Mormon Mafia” conspiracy going viral after a YouTuber investigating a stolen Lego collection flees to Mexico  
  • Behold the most random tech leak of the year involving the boss of Borderlands 4
  • Metal Gear Solid 4 was a warning

Watch this:



https://kotaku.com/fallout-76-is-finally-getting-a-next-gen-upgrade-for-ps5-and-xbox-series-x-s-nearly-eight-years-later-2000701619
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: It Looks Like The Tomb Raider Remake Might Have Gotten Delayed
item.content:encoded:
Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis, an upcoming remake of the original Tomb Raider game, just got an unceremonious drop of new screenshots thanks to PlayStation. Courtesy of a quickly removed Amazon listing, it also just got a release date, which sure sounds like something that you’d expect developer Crystal Dynamics to have said something about if it was meant to come out when it did. That’s especially true since the date is next year, much later than the game’s originally projected 2026 release.

Amazon’s listing indicated that Legacy of Atlantis is coming out on February 10, 2027. Given that Amazon is publishing the game, I would be surprised if it had bad info on when the game is coming out. The listing also included the contents found within its $70 deluxe edition, which is mostly just cosmetics, but also a “post-launch DLC story pack.”

The odd drop of information with zero fanfare has folks wondering if Legacy of Atlantis might make an appearance at tonight’s State of Play presentation where, thus far, only Insomniac’s Wolverine game is confirmed to be making an appearance. So maybe we’ll know for sure if the remake has slipped into next year before the end of the night.

This hypothetical delay does raise questions about the also-in-development Tomb Raider: Catalyst, which is supposed to launch next year. Could it have also been pushed, or are Lara Croft fans getting a double dose in 2027?



https://kotaku.com/tomb-raider-remake-legacy-atlantis-delay-release-date-2000701611
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Mina The Hollower Dev Says They’ve ‘Talked A Lot About What Mina 2 Would Be’
item.content:encoded:
Mina the Hollower just launched last week after over four years in development, so it’s perhaps a little too soon to be worrying about things like “DLC” and “sequels.” But  given that developer Yacht Club Games wasn’t able to start Mina until it was done with ten years of free DLC support for its previous title, Shovel Knight, I felt I had to ask them about what was next when I had the chance.

“No free DLC,” programmer David D’Angelo said immediately when I asked him what they learned from Shovel Knight’s Kickstarter and release that they then applied to Mina. “Basically, we’re not promising anything that—Whatever we promise is what we want to do for sure, without question. We came up with a plan for what the game was. And that’s what we put on Kickstarter. And that’s what we delivered at the end of the day. So, yeah, it was just like, no empty…or I don’t know what you even call that, like a promise that you’re not really sure what it is.”

D’Angelo is referring to the many, many stretch goals achieved by the original Shovel Knight Kickstarter campaign that then turned into ten years of work on essentially free content for the studio. Initially, those goals were just listed vaguely as stuff like, “Playable Boss Knight” and “4 Player Battle Mode.” Over time, these evolved into three entirely new campaigns for three of the original game’s enemies, plus several re-releases with new features, and a number of other free updates with things like a body swap mode, a challenge mode, and more. Yacht Club was still releasing new Shovel Knight content as recently as 2024, with 20 playable characters, online multiplayer, rewind, save states, and other new features. That’s nuts for a game that was once just sort of a bouncy, single-player Mega Man-type thing.

Not so for Mina. D’Angelo isn’t taking DLC off the table entirely, and does clarify that the team has some quality-of-life updates planned as well as a randomizer mode. “But like, we’ve literally never talked about [major DLC] or thought about it. And like, the game is huge and big. I don’t even know, we’d have to really figure out even how to add something to it.”

However, D’Angelo does say that Yacht Club is keen on spending more time with Mina. So while he’s not announcing anything at the moment, yes, the team has talked about a potential sequel. A lot.

“We’ve got pretty clear ideas, and we’ve put hints in Mina about where it could go a little bit. No one’s going to pick those up. But if we did make Mina 2, it’d be like, ‘Oh, they put that in there on purpose, right?’ But, yeah. In a couple weeks, we’ll find out how everyone’s feeling about everything, but my guess is everyone will want to take a break from Mina. But that said, we think of Mina like Shovel Knight. It’s a tentpole game of our studio that we [want to] keep going.”

Mina the Hollower has already sold 300,000 copies in three days according to Bloomberg, and is thus far the best-reviewed game of the year (I certainly loved it). Its co-founders are hoping to see that number climb significantly higher, and D’Angelo says sales of Mina will play a major role in what the studio does next. “If we sell a couple hundred thousand, we’re good to make a certain kind of game. And if we sell millions of copies, we’re good to make a different kind of game. Or be a certain size of a studio or what have you. We’re hoping it’s a Shovel Knight-style game in the end [Shovel Knight had sold 2.65 million copies as of September 2019]. That’s what has kept us going. But that said, even if it sold half as many units as Shovel Knight, maybe Mina makes Shovel Knight sell more units.”

Sales figures obviously matter for every studio, but it’s especially critical for Yacht Club, which has determinedly embraced its own independence from the beginning and refused to take funding from major publishers. That’s one of the reasons why Yacht Club went to Kickstarter to begin with, and that’s why it returned to Kickstarter for Mina. “When you take other people’s money, it comes with baggage, right?” says D’Angelo. “So unless someone is like, ‘We’re going to give you money and there’s no strings attached,’ right? Then we’re like, ‘Oh, we’re getting interested.'”



https://kotaku.com/mina-the-hollower-devs-sequel-2000701582
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
